steps.title: Check the vertex location
steps.content:
Each choice opens upward. For $y=ax^2+bx+c$, the vertex has $x$-coordinate $-\frac{b}{2a}$. With $b=-11$,
$$
-\frac{-11}{2(2)}=\frac{11}{4}=2.75
$$
This is near the scatterplot's minimum, so keep the choices with $-11x$.

steps.title: Use the y intercept
steps.content:
The scatterplot crosses the $y$-axis near $20$. Of the remaining choices, only
$$
y=2x^2-11x+20
$$
has $y$-intercept $20$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the model and compare it with the scatterplot. It has the right intercept and vertex location for the data. The answer is B.
